name: Irish Yeast Bread Mix
recipeInstructions:
1. To make the mix, combine all ingredients except lemon zest in a plastic bag.
2. Add the lemon zest and shake the bag well to ensure it's mixed evenly.
3. Seal the bag and set aside until ready to use.
4. To prepare for 1 loaf, sprinkle yeast over warm water and let it foam for 5 minutes.
5. In a large bowl, add 2 cups of the well-shaken mix along with milk, butter, and the yeast mixture.
6. Beat everything together using an electric mixer until well blended.
7. Cover the bowl and let the mixture rise in a warm place for 30 minutes.
8. Add two more cups of the mix to the dough surface in the bowl. Beat again or knead by hand until smooth and elastic, adding more flour if needed to create a stiff but pliable dough.
9. Place some flour on a work surface and fold in the raisins and currants.
10. Incorporate this into the dough and knead until everything is well combined.
11. Turn the dough out onto a greased bowl and coat it evenly.
12. Cover and let the dough rise to double its size, about 1 to 2 hours.
13. Punch down the dough and shape it into a round loaf.
14. Place on a baking sheet and allow to rise again until doubled in size.
15. Bake at 350°F until cooked through, approximately 50 minutes.
16. Makes 1 large loaf.
